trust_report.doctrine: null-over-guess; deterministic fields anchored to CDS/Scorecard with provenance; high-stakes fields blind-verified; arithmetic-gated; every source consulted is logged

overall_acceptance_rate.source: MIT Common Data Set 2024-2025 (C1) / College Scorecard
overall_acceptance_rate.quote: CDS C1 Total admitted: 1,284 of Total applicants: 28,232 [computed 1,284/28,232 = 4.55%]; College Scorecard latest.admissions.admission_rate.overall = 0.0455
overall_acceptance_rate.as_of_cycle: Fall 2024 (CDS 2024-2025 / Scorecard latest); target Fall 2027 CDS not yet published

transfer_acceptance_rate.source: MIT Common Data Set 2024-2025 (Section D, Transfers)
transfer_acceptance_rate.quote: Section D Transfer applicants: 1,346; Transfer applicants admitted: 32 [computed 32/1,346 = 2.38%]

applications_total.quote: C1 first-time, first-year applicants — Men: 17,210; Women: 8,975; Another gender: 997; Gender unknown: 1,050; Total: 28,232

admits_total.quote: C1 admitted — Men: 606; Women: 612; Another gender: 36; Gender unknown: 30; Total: 1,284

enrolled_total.quote: C1 enrolled — Men: 547; Women: 499; Another gender: 34; Gender unknown: 26; Total: 1,106

yield_rate.quote: C1 Total enrolled: 1,106; Total admitted: 1,284 [computed yield 1,106/1,284 = 86.1%]

admits_class_size.quote: Interpreted as entering first-year class size = C1 Total enrolled first-time first-years: 1,106 (Total admitted for reference: 1,284)

sat_reading_middle_50.quote: C9 SAT Evidence-Based Reading and Writing: 25th=740, 50th=760, 75th=780

test_submission_rate.quote: C9 Submitting SAT Scores: 83% (918 students); Submitting ACT Scores: 29% (323 students) — value shown is the SAT submission rate; some students submitted both

race_white.quote: B2 White, non-Hispanic: 964 of Total degree-seeking undergraduates 4,535 [computed 964/4,535 = 21.3%]; CDS B2 prints counts, not percentages

race_black.quote: B2 Black or African American, non-Hispanic: 349 of 4,535 [computed 349/4,535 = 7.7%]

race_native_american.quote: B2 American Indian or Alaska Native, non-Hispanic: 9 of 4,535 [computed 9/4,535 = 0.2%]

race_pacific_islander.quote: B2 Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander, non-Hispanic: 2 of 4,535 [computed 2/4,535 = 0.04%]

race_two_or_more.quote: B2 Two or more races, non-Hispanic: 310 of 4,535 [computed 310/4,535 = 6.8%]

international_percentage.quote: B2 Nonresident (aliens): 529 of Total degree-seeking undergraduates 4,535 [computed 529/4,535 = 11.7%]

freshman_retention_rate.quote: B22 first-year retention rate: 99.0% of full-time, first-time bachelor's degree-seeking students (Fall 2023 cohort) retained to Fall 2024

graduation_rate_4_year.source: MIT Common Data Set 2024-2025 (Section B) / College Scorecard
graduation_rate_4_year.quote: CDS four-year completion: 921 of 1,113 (2018 entering cohort) = 82.75%; College Scorecard latest.completion.completion_rate_4yr_100nt = 0.8275

graduation_rate_6_year.source: MIT Common Data Set 2024-2025 (Section B) / College Scorecard
graduation_rate_6_year.quote: CDS: 'Six-year graduation rate for 2018 cohort (G divided by C) ... 96%'; College Scorecard latest.completion.completion_rate_4yr_150nt = 0.9641

in_state_tuition.quote: G Tuition: $64,310 (2025-2026 charges). MIT is private; in-state and out-of-state tuition are identical.

total_coa_in_state.source: U.S. Dept of Education College Scorecard
total_coa_in_state.quote: College Scorecard latest.cost.attendance.academic_year = 82730 (average annual total cost of attendance). Note: CDS G lists components for 2025-26 (tuition $64,310 + fees $420 + room/board $21,264 + books $910 + other $2,436) but prints no single COA total.

total_coa_out_of_state.source: U.S. Dept of Education College Scorecard
total_coa_out_of_state.quote: College Scorecard latest.cost.attendance.academic_year = 82730. MIT is private; out-of-state COA equals in-state.

median_earnings_10yr.source: U.S. Dept of Education College Scorecard
median_earnings_10yr.quote: College Scorecard latest.earnings.10_yrs_after_entry.median = 143372 (median earnings of former students 10 years after entry)

is_test_optional.quote: C8: 'SAT or ACT' marked 'Required to be considered for admission' (latest submission Feb. 15). MIT is neither test-optional nor test-blind.

test_policy_details.value: SAT or ACT required for first-year applicants (not test-optional or test-blind); latest test date accepted Feb. 15
test_policy_details.quote: C8: 'SAT or ACT' Required to be considered for admission; latest date for test scores: Feb. 15

waitlist_offered.quote: C2 Number of students offered a place on the waitlist: 590 (waitlist not ranked)

waitlist_accepted.quote: C2 Number accepting a place on the waitlist: 509

waitlist_admitted.quote: C2 Number of waitlisted students admitted: 9

waitlist_admit_rate.quote: C2 Admitted from waitlist: 9 of 509 who accepted a waitlist place [computed 9/509 = 1.77%]

notes: MIT 2024-25 CDS project page — most recent edition; data presented inline on the page, no separate downloadable PDF/xlsx file link found.

Primary source. Pulled C1 (apps/admits/enrolled), C2 (waitlist), C8 (test policy), C9 (SAT/ACT/submission), B2 (race counts), B22 (retention), B (4/6-yr grad), G (tuition/cost), D (transfers), C21/C22 (ED=No, EA=Yes but counts not reported).
CDS C1 Total admitted: 1,284 of Total applicants: 28,232 [computed 1,284/28,232 = 4.55%]; College Scorecard latest.admissions.admission_rate.overall = 0.0455
MIT does not report first-year acceptance rate broken out by state residency in the CDS or Scorecard.
Section D Transfer applicants: 1,346; Transfer applicants admitted: 32 [computed 32/1,346 = 2.38%]
MIT does not report acceptance rate for international applicants in the CDS or Scorecard.

Section index page; not separately fetched — its child pages (Advanced Placement, International Examinations) carry the authoritative detail.

PRIMARY AP source. Fetched twice for verbatim text: AP general rule (score of 5, no partial credit, no credit for secondary-school AP courses), all 'No credit is given for...' sentences (Biology, Chemistry, Computer Science, Physics 1/2 separately, Environmental Science, Statistics), Calculus BC->18.01, Calculus AB->18.01A/18.02A, Physics C->8.01, HASS-Requirement usage sentence, unrestricted-electives definition, and the Class of 2030 (Fall 2026) effective statement.

PRIMARY IB source. Verbatim: AS-Level exams get NO credit, HL score of 7 -> 18.01 and 8.01, HL humanities 7 -> 9 units unrestricted elective, English HL 7 -> CI-H/CI-HW Required without First Year Essay Evaluation, no credit for IB Biology/Chemistry/ITGS, and confirmation there is no IB diploma bonus.
First-Year office transfer-credit page; the registrar page was used as the authoritative transfer source instead.
Procedural how-to; process details already captured verbatim from the registrar page.

Confirms application component list (bio, essays/activities/academics, transcripts, letters, tests, interview, creative portfolios, Feb Updates) and the Nov 1 / Jan 5 deadlines.

Interview policy: offered 'whenever possible' via MIT Educational Council (3,500+ alumni volunteers); 'If we are unable to offer you an interview, it will be waived and your application will not be adversely affected.'
Optional creative portfolios (Research supplement, Music & Theater Arts, Visual Art & Architecture, Maker) via SlideRoom, $10 with waivers, same deadline as application cycle.
Testing policy: 'We require the SAT or the ACT'; ACT writing / SAT essay not required; superscored; self-reported; EA before Nov 30 / RA before Dec 31.

Primary source. Verbatim: 'When you apply to MIT, you apply to the entire university, not to a specific major or school, so all first-year students begin MIT undeclared'; 'you are then free to choose from among any of MIT's courses of study, without any additional requirements or admission procedures.' Basis for admits_by_major, tactics 2 and 4, and alternate_major_strategy.

Official MIT Admissions FAQ. Corroborated the Arts/English/History & Social Sciences/World Languages 9-units-each unrestricted-elective sentence and the Capstone Seminar+Research 9-unit rule that appeared in search snippets.

Confirms EA 'November 1' / RA 'January 5'; test cutoffs 'any test taken before November 30' (EA) and 'before December 31' (RA).

PRIMARY transfer source. financial-aid page
MIT Student Financial Services undergraduate page — used as financial-aid URL; states MIT meets 100% of demonstrated need, need-blind.

Anchor/landing page. Sourced need-blind admission, meet-full-need-for-four-years, definition of full need (100% via scholarships/grants/employment), need-based framing, sub-page links, and the 88%-debt-free (2025) and sub-$200K-tuition-free statements.

Only yielded the mission statement ('ensure an MIT education is affordable to every admitted student...regardless of financial circumstances'); other specifics were on linked pages. Minor corroboration.
PRIMARY value source: 'Tuition: $66,720' for the 2026–2027 academic year; full 2026-27 cost-of-attendance breakdown totaling $92,760 before aid.

Tuition: $66,720 [for the 2026–2027 academic year] (MIT SFS Annual student budget). Corroborated by MIT Registrar: "Full regular tuition" of "$33,360" per term (fall + spring = $66,720) for 2026-2027.

Authoritative affordability page: $200K tuition-free (2025-2026 onward), sub-$100K pay-nothing, need-blind AND full-need for all domestic+international undergrads, no-loan calculation statement, 57% MIT Scholarship recipients (2024-2025), 88% graduate debt-free.
